requirements.title: Pack submission initiation
requirements.text_ieee830: The Workbench shall accept applicant submission of an Operational Authorisation Pack via web portal, with drag-and-drop file upload capability for artefacts (PDF, XLSX, DOCX format). Upon submission, the Workbench shall timestamp the submission and initiate Artefact Detector component.

requirements.title: Pack artefact detection (automated)
requirements.text_ieee830: The Artefact Detector shall parse submitted files and identify document type (ConOps, SORA, Safety Case, etc.) via filename pattern matching and header inspection. For each identified artefact, the system shall record presence/absence, file size, and page count. The system shall output a RAG matrix (Red/Amber/Green status) per artefact against mandatory-artefact list.

requirements.title: Pack completeness assessment (Quick Gate)
requirements.text_ieee830: The Workbench shall, within 10 minutes of submission, run Quick Gate (METER-001): Artefact Detector maps identified files to 8 mandatory artefacts and generates RAG matrix. If any artefact is Red (missing) or Amber (present but incomplete per page-count thresholds: ConOps >= 5 pages, SORA >= 3 pages, Safety Case >= 10 pages, etc.), Quick Gate FAILS and case enters 'return-for-clarification' state. If all artefacts are Green, Quick Gate PASSES and case advances to Thorough Gate.

requirements.title: Pack completeness assessment (Thorough Gate)
requirements.text_ieee830: Upon Quick Gate PASS, case is assigned to CAA case officer who conducts Thorough Gate (METER-002): manual section-by-section audit of each artefact (ConOps sections: swarm composition, phases, failure modes, worst-credible contingencies; SORA sections: hazard tree, swarm interdependencies, controls; Safety Case sections: assumption validation, argument sufficiency, evidence cross-references). Case officer marks each section Present-Substantive, Present-Vague, or Absent. If >= 90% sections are Present-Substantive, Thorough Gate PASSES; case advances to SORA Assessment panel.

requirements.title: Return-for-clarification notification
requirements.text_ieee830: If Quick Gate or Thorough Gate fails, Workbench shall notify applicant within 1 business day with itemized list of missing/incomplete artefacts by category (ConOps section X, SORA missing entirely, Safety Case section Y too brief, etc.). Applicant shall have 14 days to resubmit revised pack.

requirements.title: Pack submission versioning
requirements.text_ieee830: The Workbench shall maintain immutable version history of all pack submissions (original submission, resubmission 1, resubmission 2, etc.). Each version shall be timestamped and stored in evidence archive with SHA256 hash. Case history shall display which version was accepted for case opening and reasons for prior rejections.

requirements.title: Case opening upon pack acceptance
requirements.text_ieee830: Upon Thorough Gate PASS, Workbench shall automatically transition case from Draft status to Open status, generate case file (unique ID, applicant name, trial location, classification level), and notify assigned SORA assessor. CAA case officer shall add case to SORA Assessment queue with estimated review date.

requirements.title: Swarm-aware SORA evidence reception
requirements.text_ieee830: SORA Assessor shall receive applicant-submitted swarm-aware SORA evidence document addressing: (1) inter-vehicle coordination dependencies (how aircraft coordinate, what happens if one fails); (2) emergent risks specific to multi-agent operation (separation-loss cascades, deadlock, Byzantine failures); (3) documented hazard analysis (FTA, STAMP, or HARA explicitly mapping swarm-specific failure modes). Evidence shall reference JARUS SORA v2.5 methodology or published swarm-aware SORA supplement (when available).

requirements.title: SORA swarm evidence validation
requirements.text_ieee830: SORA Assessor shall evaluate whether swarm evidence defensibly addresses: (1) JARUS v2.5 methodology applied to swarm context (hazard identification, risk classification); (2) inter-aircraft failure dependencies documented (does failure of aircraft A increase risk to aircraft B?); (3) controls proposed for identified swarm-specific hazards (formation separation enforcement, leader loss recovery, Byzantine detection). Assessor shall issue: ACCEPT (evidence sufficient), RFC (return for clarification with specific gaps), or REFUSE (evidence fundamentally inadequate).

requirements.title: SAIL/OSO determination
requirements.text_ieee830: Upon swarm evidence ACCEPT, SORA Assessor shall determine Specific Assurance and Integrity Level (SAIL) and Operational Safety Objectives (OSO) that explicitly reference swarm coordination constraints. Example OSO: 'Separation maintained during coordinated evasion maneuver shall be >= 50 meters horizontally and 20 meters vertically; formation shall not deadlock (all aircraft movement ceases) for > 10 seconds.' SAIL and OSO shall be documented and forwarded to safety-case reviewer.

requirements.title: ConOps swarm composition specification
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall provide ConOps document specifying: (1) swarm composition (number of aircraft, types, roles: lead, follower, observer); (2) communication architecture (mesh, star, hierarchical); (3) decision authority per operational phase (who decides: ground crew, lead aircraft, peer-vote consensus?). Safety-case reviewer shall validate that roles, communication, and authority match swarm decision-making architecture (REQ008).

requirements.title: ConOps operational phases
requirements.text_ieee830: ConOps shall specify operational phases with crew decision points: launch (pre-flight checks, system arm), transit (waypoint navigation, formation hold), contingency (loss of leader, C2 loss, encounter evasion), recovery (descent, landing, system disarm). For each phase, identify nominal behavior thresholds and crew intervention triggers.

requirements.title: Worst-credible behavior coverage
requirements.text_ieee830: Safety-case reviewer shall validate that ConOps explicitly addresses all worst-credible behavior scenarios (most severe single-failure or environmental excursion reasonably foreseeable). Worst-credible scenarios include: GPS loss, C2 link loss, geofence breach, airspace intrusion, low battery, crew incapacitation. For each scenario, ConOps shall specify crew procedure or containment system response that prevents safety-critical outcome.

requirements.title: ODD boundary specification
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall provide Operational Design Domain (ODD) specifying operating conditions: wind speed <= 15 knots, temperature -5°C to +30°C, GPS accuracy better than 5 meters, daylight hours only (or night operations if equipped with lighting). ODD defines the envelope within which swarm behavior is predictable per ConOps and autonomy architecture.

requirements.title: TDA 3D airspace definition
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall submit Temporary Danger Area (TDA) request to Airspace Regulator specifying 3D airspace volume: (1) latitude/longitude polygon (trial corridor); (2) altitude bands (floor and ceiling AGL); (3) time window (date range, daylight hours, nights if applicable). TDA shall be formatted per ICAO Annex 15 NOTAM requirements.

requirements.title: TDA consultation evidence
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall provide evidence of consultation with: (1) ATSU (Air Traffic Service Unit) confirming TDA monitoring capability and NOTAM publication; (2) airspace users (e.g., commercial routes, helicopter operators) acknowledging TDA constraints; (3) affected landowners (property owners below trial corridor) with notice of flight operations. Consultation documentation shall include dates of contact, responses received, and any conditions imposed by consulted parties.

requirements.title: Geofence boundary tolerance specification
requirements.text_ieee830: The Workbench geofence monitor shall define 3D boundary with tolerance ±10 meters (i.e., aircraft position may drift 10m beyond advertised boundary before containment triggers). This tolerance accounts for GPS uncertainty (±5m typical), wind gust deflection, and sensor latency. Boundary coordinates shall be entered with confidence interval (e.g., 'boundary 51.3°N ± 0.001°, aircraft position must remain within this latitude').

requirements.title: Geofence breach detection
requirements.text_ieee830: Onboard geofence monitor shall poll GNSS at 10 Hz minimum. If aircraft position exceeds boundary by > 10 meters for > 2 seconds (hysteresis filter to avoid false triggers), system shall declare geofence-breach condition and initiate containment action within 500 milliseconds.

requirements.title: Primary containment action (Return-to-Launch)
requirements.text_ieee830: Upon geofence breach detection, onboard autonomy shall prioritize Return-to-Launch (RTL) if GPS signal confidence > 95%. RTL shall compute safe descent path to final landing location and execute maneuver autonomously (without requiring ground crew input). RTL shall respect ODD boundaries (wind limits, terrain clearance).

requirements.title: Failover containment action (climb-and-hold)
requirements.text_ieee830: If GPS confidence drops below 95% (signal loss), onboard autonomy shall execute failover: climb to 500 feet AGL minimum and maintain level flight (no descent) until GPS recovers or crew intervention occurs. Failover preserves safe altitude above terrain while awaiting GPS recovery or crew manual landing.

requirements.title: Crew containment alert
requirements.text_ieee830: Upon geofence breach detection, Workbench shall generate crew alert (audio tone 60+ dB, visual indicator on HUD) within 1 second of breach. Alert shall indicate breach location (direction and distance from boundary) and containment action initiated (RTL or climb-and-hold). Crew shall acknowledge alert and monitor containment action completion.

requirements.title: C2 link quality telemetry
requirements.text_ieee830: Spectrum Regulator shall authorize C2 link frequency allocation and power limits. Applicant shall instrument C2 system to continuously transmit real-time link quality telemetry (RSSI: received signal strength indicator in dBm, BER: bit error rate as percentage) to ground crew display and Workbench logger. Link quality shall be recorded at minimum 1 Hz sampling rate.

requirements.title: C2 link loss detection
requirements.text_ieee830: C2 link loss shall be detected when signal quality (RSSI or BER) drops below minimum threshold for > 1 second. Minimum threshold shall be determined during pre-trial testing and documented in trial authorization. Upon detection, onboard autonomy shall execute failover: if inter-vehicle links available, swarm coordinates evasion autonomously; if inter-vehicle links also unavailable, each aircraft independently executes Return-to-Launch.

requirements.title: C2 link survivability demonstration
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall demonstrate through simulation and flight test that swarm remains safe (maintains separation, respects geofence, avoids collision) for minimum 5 minutes after C2 link loss, sufficient for crew to re-establish link or initiate independent termination. Survivability test shall document: time until GPS-based navigation fails (if applicable), time until battery/fuel margin insufficient for RTL, time until crew manual takeover required.

requirements.title: Inter-vehicle link coordination
requirements.text_ieee830: For swarm coordination during C2 loss, inter-vehicle links shall provide real-time peer-to-peer communication (aircraft-to-aircraft): position sharing (each aircraft broadcasts location and velocity), decision coordination (consensus protocol for avoiding evasion conflicts), heartbeat (periodic 'alive' signal to detect failed aircraft). Inter-vehicle link design and frequency allocation shall be approved by Spectrum Regulator and documented in trial authorization.

requirements.title: DAA sensor detection range
requirements.text_ieee830: Each aircraft in swarm shall be equipped with onboard Detect-and-Avoid (DAA) sensor (radar or electro-optical) with minimum detection range 500 meters, update rate 1 Hz. DAA sensor shall operate independent of C2 link (autonomous on-aircraft function). Applicant shall provide evidence of DAA sensor performance (detection probability, false-alarm rate) per DO-365 Minimum Operational Performance Standards.

requirements.title: Cooperative encounter resolution
requirements.text_ieee830: For cooperative encounters (intruder transmits ADS-B or FLARM signal indicating position/velocity), swarm de-confliction algorithm shall: (1) receive intruder position via sensor and broadcast data, (2) compute relative bearing and closing rate, (3) select recommended avoidance maneuver (climb, descend, turn left/right) that provides minimum 100-meter separation, (4) broadcast maneuver to all swarm aircraft via inter-vehicle links, (5) enforce agreed maneuver execution within 2 seconds.

requirements.title: Non-cooperative encounter resolution
requirements.text_ieee830: For non-cooperative encounters (intruder does not transmit ADS-B/FLARM), DAA system shall: (1) detect intruder via radar/EO sensor, (2) compute relative motion, (3) issue crew-advisory maneuver (preferred direction and magnitude to achieve 100-meter separation), (4) record decision rationale and alternative maneuvers considered in log. Crew shall evaluate advisory and execute manually or allow autonomous execution per trial procedures.

requirements.title: Encounter resolution predictability
requirements.text_ieee830: Encounter resolution is predictable when: (1) same encounter geometry (relative bearing, range, closing rate) always produces same recommended maneuver (deterministic, not probabilistic); (2) all swarm aircraft follow recommended maneuver within 2-second execution window (no divergence); (3) resulting maneuver is foreseeable to all trial participants (documented in operational procedures). Non-deterministic (ML-based, probabilistic) encounter algorithms are not acceptable unless validated against 1000+ diverse encounter scenarios with 99%+ determinism rate.

requirements.title: Encounter resolution failure criteria
requirements.text_ieee830: Failure conditions for encounter resolution: collision (aircraft or swarm member separation < 50 meters), loss of separation (separation < 100 meters between any two aircraft or between aircraft and intruder), crew inability to monitor encounter outcome. Upon any failure, trial shall be immediately suspended and incident investigation initiated (REQ012).

requirements.title: Swarm decision-making architecture options
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall document swarm decision-making architecture and justify selection of one of three design patterns: (1) Centralized (ground crew or ground-based AI agent makes all swarm decisions, aircraft execute commands), (2) Distributed (aircraft share state via inter-vehicle links, peer-vote consensus selects action), (3) Hierarchical (lead aircraft decides, follower aircraft execute). Each architecture has different failure modes and requires different assurance evidence (REQ008).

requirements.title: Swarm decision authority specification
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall explicitly specify which entity has decision authority in swarm: (1) ground crew (crew commands transmitted via C2), (2) onboard autonomy (programmed decision rules), (3) peer consensus (aircraft vote on action). Authority shall be defined per operational scenario (nominal, loss of leader, link loss, encounter evasion). Autonomy reviewer shall verify authority specification is consistent with meaningful human control requirement (REQ010).

requirements.title: Swarm communication topology
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall document swarm communication topology: (1) mesh (all aircraft communicate with all others — high redundancy, higher latency), (2) star (all aircraft communicate with lead aircraft — lower latency, single-point-of-failure if lead loses comms), (3) line (leader to second, second to third, etc. — serial communication, longest latency). Topology choice affects resilience to inter-vehicle link loss and shall be justified in architecture specification.

requirements.title: Emergent behavior failure modes
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall identify potential emergent behaviors (unintended collective behaviors arising from inter-aircraft interactions): formation deadlock (all aircraft movement ceases), oscillation (formation bounces up-down-up-down without damping), cascade separation-loss (one aircraft's evasion maneuver causes follower to lose separation, triggering second evasion, widening gap). For each identified emergent-failure mode, applicant shall propose detection mechanism (onboard monitoring, ground observation, automated alerting) and recovery procedure.

requirements.title: Emergent behavior verification evidence
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall provide emergent-behavior evidence through three methods: (1) Formal Verification (model checking or theorem proving proving absence of undesired emergent behaviors in state-machine representation), (2) Monte Carlo Simulation (minimum 1,000 trials per scenario with environmental noise injection, demonstrating behavior variance bounds), (3) HITL Testing (hardware-in-the-loop simulation with human operators testing formation behaviors, contingency responses, loss-of-leader recovery). Results shall bound probability of undesired emergent behaviors (e.g., 'deadlock occurs in < 0.1% of trials; separation-loss cascade depth bounded at 2 aircraft').

requirements.title: AMLAS case structure
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall provide Assurance Case for Machine Learning in Autonomous Systems (AMLAS) per Assure ML CTC (www.assuranceml.org). AMLAS case shall document: (1) safety requirements levied on ML components (e.g., 'swarm de-confliction must execute in <500ms with <1% error rate'), (2) training data governance (data source, labeling process, distribution verification), (3) model architecture (layers, parameters, non-linearities), (4) robustness testing (adversarial examples, sensor-noise injection, out-of-distribution detection), (5) failure modes (model confidence collapse, out-of-distribution triggers, performance degradation under environmental stress). AMLAS case structure shall follow GSN (Goal Structured Notation) or CAF (Confidence Assurance Framework).

requirements.title: Frozen-model manifest documentation
requirements.text_ieee830: For each ML model deployed in trial, applicant shall provide Frozen-Model Manifest documenting: (1) model version ID and release date, (2) training dataset hash value (SHA256) enabling post-incident reconstruction, (3) hyperparameters (learning rate, regularization, architecture details), (4) performance metrics (validation accuracy, F1 score, confusion matrix for safety-critical decision classes), (5) operational constraints (input domain thresholds, confidence thresholds for model predictions), (6) explicit immutability confirmation (model weights and architecture shall remain unchanged throughout trial duration, minimum 2 years). Manifest shall be encrypted and stored with copies held by CAA and applicant.

requirements.title: ML model explainability techniques
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall provide post-hoc explainability evidence demonstrating sufficient transparency for forensic investigation. For deep learning models: SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) analysis showing feature importance contributions to individual decisions, decision-tree approximations of learned decision boundaries, or mechanistic interpretability exploration. For traditional ML (decision trees, linear models): direct interpretation of model structure. Forensic explainability does not require perfect reconstruction of neural-network internals but must enable post-incident analysis: identify which inputs triggered anomalous output, determine if model was operating within training-distribution, link decision to model version per Frozen-Model Manifest.

requirements.title: Crew situational awareness update frequency
requirements.text_ieee830: Crew shall receive swarm state updates (position, airspeed, altitude, link status, autonomous decisions of each swarm member) at minimum 1 Hz frequency via HUD, dashboard, or voice notification. Update frequency shall be sufficient for crew to maintain continuous situational awareness and intervene before safety-critical outcomes (> 5-second intervention time window per REQ010).

requirements.title: Crew override authority and latency
requirements.text_ieee830: Crew shall retain authority to override autonomous decisions at all times. Override mechanism shall include: (1) dedicated control input (buttons, switches, voice command) for safety-critical functions (swarm termination, return-to-launch, emergency descent), (2) override latency < 2 seconds from crew command to system response (e.g., crew presses 'STOP SWARM' button; within 2 seconds, all aircraft engines cut or parachutes deploy). Latency shall be verified through HITL testing and flight test with independent observer timing crew response.

requirements.title: Crew intervention time windows
requirements.text_ieee830: Crew shall have minimum intervention time windows to perceive off-nominal events and intervene before safety-critical outcomes: (1) >= 5 seconds for evasion maneuvers (crew detects threat via DAA warning, selects maneuver direction, system executes), (2) >= 10 seconds for return-to-launch initiation (crew recognizes deteriorating conditions, decides RTL is necessary, system begins descent), (3) >= 2 seconds for emergency termination (crew recognizes uncontrolled situation, activates independent termination, system cuts motors). Intervention time windows shall be validated through simulator testing and worst-credible-scenario analysis (ConOps REQ003).

requirements.title: Crew workload assessment (NASA-TLX)
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall conduct pre-trial workload assessment using NASA-TLX (Task Load Index) or equivalent cognitive-load measurement instrument. Assessment shall measure crew workload in simulator scenarios representing: (1) nominal operations (formation flight, waypoint navigation), (2) contingency operations (loss-of-leader recovery, C2 link loss, encounter evasion). Target workload: <75% of maximum cognitive capacity during nominal operations, <85% during contingency. Results shall be documented and submitted to CAA Human Factors Inspector for approval before trial start.

requirements.title: Crew take-over simulator practice
requirements.text_ieee830: Crew shall complete minimum 10 practice take-over sessions in HITL simulator covering: (1) assumed-attitude recovery (crew receives blind gyro indicator and must regain awareness of swarm formation state within 30 seconds), (2) formation-separation recovery (crew detects separation loss and must issue manual maneuvers to restore spacing), (3) autonomous-decision-override (crew overrides autonomy system and manually commands swarm to new waypoint). Each session shall be logged with date, scenario, and instructor evaluation (pass/fail). Instructor shall confirm crew achieves >= 95% success rate across all 10 sessions before trial approval.

requirements.title: Telemetry recording (aircraft state)
requirements.text_ieee830: Workbench shall record aircraft state telemetry at 10 Hz minimum throughout each trial flight: position (latitude, longitude, altitude), velocity (north/east/down), attitude (roll, pitch, yaw), acceleration (linear and angular), engine thrust (or propeller RPM), battery voltage and current. All samples shall be timestamped (GPS time reference, nanosecond precision) and logged to tamper-evident container (encrypted storage with separate integrity hash).

requirements.title: Telemetry recording (autonomy decisions)
requirements.text_ieee830: Workbench shall record autonomy decision logs at 1 Hz minimum: command issued (waypoint, descent rate, evasion maneuver), decision rationale (rule or ML-model inference leading to command), confidence score (if applicable), alternative commands considered and rejected. Logs shall enable post-incident forensics: determine why system issued a particular command and whether command was appropriate given system state.

requirements.title: Telemetry recording (link quality)
requirements.text_ieee830: Workbench shall record C2 and inter-vehicle link quality at 1 Hz minimum: RSSI (received signal strength indicator, dBm), BER (bit error rate, percentage), packet loss rate, latency (round-trip time). Logs shall enable post-incident forensics: determine if link was degrading before failure event, whether degradation triggered failover behavior, whether crew was aware of deteriorating link quality.

requirements.title: Telemetry recording (crew actions)
requirements.text_ieee830: Workbench shall log all crew inputs with timestamp: control inputs (buttons, switches, stick movements), verbal commands (radio transmissions, voice control activations), alert acknowledgments (crew responding to Workbench warnings). Crew voice logs shall be encrypted and treated as sensitive personal data (GDPR compliant): retained only if incident occurs; deleted 90 days post-flight if no incident. Crew shall be informed at mission briefing that voice is being recorded for safety investigation purposes only.

requirements.title: Telemetry recording (video & audio)
requirements.text_ieee830: Workbench shall record: (1) forward-looking video from lead aircraft (minimum 30 fps, minimum 1080p resolution) showing flight path and adjacent airspace for traffic awareness, (2) downlink video from ground-based operator camera (if applicable, showing trial area from ground perspective), (3) intercom audio from crew (minimum 16 kHz sampling, stereo for multi-crew operations). Video/audio logs shall be time-synchronized to telemetry via GPS time-of-day stamp, enabling correlation of crew actions with aircraft state during incident investigation.

requirements.title: MOR trigger identification
requirements.text_ieee830: MOR (Mandatory Occurrence Report) shall be filed upon detection of reportable events: (1) accident (any unplanned impact causing injury or structural damage), (2) serious incident (near-miss with < 100-meter separation from third-party aircraft or ground infrastructure), (3) loss-of-separation event (swarm member separation < 100 meters unintentionally), (4) unintended geofence breach (aircraft exits TDA boundary outside planned experiment), (5) autonomous-system anomaly (behavior inconsistent with documented design, indicative of control-system defect), (6) crew incapacitation (medical event disabling crew during operations), (7) unrecovered off-nominal event (crew unable to recover from failure scenario within expected time window). Event triggers shall be pre-briefed to crew and ground observers.

requirements.title: MOR compilation and submission
requirements.text_ieee830: Upon MOR-reportable event detection, applicant shall compile incident report within 72 hours containing: (1) incident narrative (what happened, when, where, who involved), (2) immediate cause (why did event occur: equipment failure, crew error, environmental factor, software defect?), (3) contributing factors (were there preceding failures or degradation?), (4) recorded evidence links (telemetry files, video, crew statement, photograph), (5) immediate corrective actions taken (swarm terminated, investigation initiated, etc.). Report shall be submitted to CAA Safety Investigation department with evidence attachment (encrypted archive containing telemetry, video, logs).

requirements.title: DPIA (Data Protection Impact Assessment)
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall conduct DPIA per GDPR Article 35, identifying: (1) personal data collected (crew names, observer identities, potentially incidental recording of ground personnel faces), (2) processing purposes (safety investigation, regulatory oversight, technical analysis), (3) data retention periods (2+ years or until investigation closes), (4) data-subject rights (right to access, right to erasure upon request after investigation closes), (5) privacy risks (accidental exposure of crew voice recordings, unintended capture of third-party ground personnel). DPIA shall document privacy controls (voice-log encryption, access restriction to authorized investigators, automated redaction of civilian faces in video before public release).

requirements.title: Insurance coverage minimum
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall obtain third-party liability insurance covering: (1) bodily injury (death or injury to ground personnel below trial corridor), (2) property damage (damage to third-party buildings, vehicles, infrastructure), (3) environmental harm (contamination, wildlife disturbance claims). Minimum coverage: 1 million GBP per occurrence, 5 million GBP annual aggregate. Insurance policy shall not exclude BVLOS or swarm operations. Certificate of insurance shall be provided to CAA prior to trial start and remain active throughout trial duration.

requirements.title: Ground environment survey scope
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall commission independent ground environment survey assessing trial area within 10 km radius of planned operations. Survey shall be conducted by licensed surveyor or environmental consultant and identify: (1) critical ground infrastructure (power lines, communication towers, railways, roads, water pipelines) with GPS coordinates and criticality classification, (2) population density (buildings, schools, hospitals, campgrounds) by district with estimated occupancy, (3) environmental sensitivity (water sources, protected habitats, cultural heritage sites) per regulatory register, (4) noise-sensitive receptors (residences, hospitals, schools) identified by occupancy type, (5) terrain features and wind patterns affecting aircraft behavior.

requirements.title: Ground survey risk mitigation
requirements.text_ieee830: For each identified ground hazard (power line within 5 km, hospital within 2 km, protected habitat nearby), applicant shall propose mitigation: (1) reduced altitude (stay above 500 feet to clear power lines), (2) flight-path adjustment (avoid corridor near hospital, use alternate route), (3) noise baffles or operational restrictions (no night operations near residential areas), (4) observer deployment (ground personnel stationed at critical points to watch for hazards). Applicant shall provide risk acceptance rationale for hazards where mitigation is not feasible, documented and approved by CAA Authorisation Officer.

requirements.title: Crew competency domains
requirements.text_ieee830: Crew shall demonstrate competency across three domains: (1) General Airmanship (private pilot license or equivalent, current medical certificate, minimum 500 flight hours VLOS or BVLOS), (2) BVLOS Swarm Operations (completion of approved training program minimum 20 hours covering swarm behavior, coordination failures, contingency procedures, manual takeover scenarios), (3) System-Specific Knowledge (completion of type-training for designated UAS model covering launch/recovery, autonomy modes, C2 management, emergency procedures). Evidence shall be documented and reviewed by CAA Licensing Inspector.

requirements.title: Crew competency evidence types
requirements.text_ieee830: Competency evidence shall consist of four types: (1) Training Records (signed certificates from approved training organization, dated within 2 years), (2) Written Examination (scored assessment minimum 80% pass rate covering swarm operations, contingency procedures, system knowledge), (3) Simulator Practice (minimum 10 logged practice sessions in HITL simulator per competency domain, each with instructor evaluation), (4) Flight Experience (logbook entries documenting BVLOS or swarm flights, signed by supervising pilot if required by regulatory authority). CAA Licensing Inspector shall review all four types and issue permit (approved), conditional permit (requires remedial training), or deny (material gaps).

requirements.title: Regulatory apportionment matrix
requirements.text_ieee830: For trials involving classified MOD evidence or military applications, applicant and CAA shall jointly develop Regulatory Apportionment Matrix specifying: (1) which requirements each authority owns (CAA: airspace segregation, civilian safety, public notification; MAA: security classification, military airspace, sensitive technology export control), (2) decision dependencies (which authority approves first, approval sequence), (3) information barriers (which evidence is classified vs. shareable), (4) escalation procedures (if CAA and MAA requirements conflict, escalate to executive decision level). Matrix shall be reviewed and signed by designated CAA and MAA representatives confirming gap-free coverage (every requirement REQ001-REQ020 assigned to at least one authority).

requirements.title: Phased trial structure
requirements.text_ieee830: Trial shall be structured in three reversible phases controlled by CAA Programme Manager: Phase 1 (VLOS Baseline) — single aircraft, visual control, crew within 500m. Gate criteria: ConOps approved, SORA assessment, insurance, crew credentials. Phase 2 (BVLOS Single) — 2+ aircraft, autonomous waypoint navigation, C2 link reliance. Gate criteria: Phase 1 success (minimum 10 flights, zero critical incidents), BVLOS-specific equipment validation, C2 link 99.5% uptime demonstrated. Phase 3 (BVLOS Swarm) — full multi-aircraft coordinated operations, inter-vehicle link coordination, distributed decision-making. Gate criteria: Phase 2 success (minimum 20 flights, zero unrecovered off-nominal events), swarm decision-making validation, emergent-behavior evidence bounding undesired behaviors to acceptable probability.

requirements.title: Phase exit criteria and reversal
requirements.text_ieee830: Phase exit/reversal triggered by: (1) >= 2 critical incidents in single phase (loss of separation <100m, uncontrolled off-nominal event crew unable to recover, containment failure), (2) failure of phase exit criteria (e.g., Phase 1 requires zero incidents in 10 flights; observing 2+ incidents triggers reversal), (3) uncontrolled emergent behavior detected (swarm deadlock, formation oscillation, Byzantine cascade). Upon reversal, Programme Manager shall: suspend flight operations immediately, conduct root-cause analysis, identify contributing factors, document remediation plan (design change, retraining, equipment replacement), execute remediation, and conduct re-entry testing before attempting same phase again.

requirements.title: Landowner notification
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall identify property owners within 2 km of trial area (via land registry) and deliver written notice minimum 30 days prior to trial start. Notice shall explain: trial objectives, aircraft specifications, estimated noise levels, flight duration, safety measures, emergency contact information. Notification shall be delivered via certified mail with proof-of-delivery signature required. Applicant shall document all notifications and proof-of-delivery certificates for CAA review.

requirements.title: Community consultation
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall conduct community consultation through: (1) public information session (or online information portal if remote area) describing trial objectives, safety measures, flight schedule, anticipated noise and visual impact, or (2) community liaison hotline accepting questions and concerns. Consultation period shall be minimum 14 days after notice delivery. Applicant shall document all concerns received and responses provided. CAA Stakeholder Engagement Lead shall review consultation documentation to verify CAP 1616 compliance.

requirements.title: Media relations and press release
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall issue press release to local media (5+ business days before trial start) describing trial objectives, aircraft, safety measures, regulatory oversight, and designated media contact for questions. Applicant shall prepare response templates for anticipated media questions (noise, safety, privacy, environmental impact). Single media contact shall be designated and trained to provide consistent messaging.

requirements.title: GDPR Privacy Notice publication
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall publish GDPR Article 13 Privacy Notice describing: personal data collected (crew names, observer identities, potentially incidental recording of nearby ground personnel), processing purposes (safety investigation, regulatory oversight, technical analysis), data-retention periods (2+ years or until investigation closes), data-subject rights (access, deletion after investigation closes), CAA Data Protection Officer contact. Privacy Notice shall be posted on Workbench portal and provided to all trial participants and affected ground parties.

requirements.title: Security Classification Handling Plan (SCHP) creation
requirements.text_ieee830: When trial involves classified MOD evidence, applicant and MAA shall jointly develop Security Classification Handling Plan per CAP 722H (CAA Security Management) and JSP 440 (UK Armed Forces Security Classification Policy). SCHP shall specify: (1) classification levels per evidence element (e.g., swarm de-confliction algorithm = OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE, MOD sensor specifications = SECRET), (2) information barriers (what evidence CAA can access unclassified vs. what requires SECRET facility and clearance), (3) redaction rules (which documents shall be redacted for CAA review), (4) facility requirements (MOD secure facility location, accreditation level), (5) personnel clearances required, (6) escalation if CAA deems safety-critical evidence restricted.

requirements.title: Parallel classified/unclassified assessment
requirements.text_ieee830: CAA shall designate Classified Evidence Reviewer (possessing required SECRET or TOP SECRET clearance) to conduct parallel assessment: (1) Classified Technical Review (full review of MOD evidence in secure facility, complete assessment of swarm autonomy, sensor integration, military interdependencies), (2) Unclassified Summary (Reviewer produces unclassified summary of findings and approval rationale safe for public record, without disclosing classified technical details). Trial authorization decision shall be issued based on unclassified summary; classified assessment remains confidential to authorized personnel only.

requirements.title: Environmental noise assessment
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall commission noise assessment measuring or modeling predicted noise levels (dB(A)) at sensitive receptors (residences, schools, hospitals) within 5 km. Assessment shall address: (1) daytime operations (0700-2200) — target maximum 75 dB(A) at residential receivers per Environmental Protection Act 1990, (2) night operations (2200-0700) — target maximum 65 dB(A). If predicted noise exceeds limits, applicant shall propose mitigation: flight-path adjustment, noise baffles, operational restrictions (daylight hours only, altitude ceiling to reduce ground-noise impact), or acceptance of higher noise with community notification and compensation.

requirements.title: Species disturbance assessment
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall commission ecological assessment identifying sensitive species (birds, bats, other protected fauna) within 10 km using published ecology databases and expert consultation. Assessment shall identify: (1) breeding seasons and dusk/night activity periods (when disturbance risk is highest), (2) nesting/foraging habitat locations (particularly for protected or endangered species), (3) UAS noise/motion disturbance impact per wildlife sensitivity guidance. If disturbance risk is elevated (e.g., active nest site within 1 km, bat foraging habitat within 2 km during breeding season), applicant shall propose mitigation (seasonal flight restrictions, dusk/night flight ban, artificial shelter deployment) or obtain Natural England license for unavoidable disturbance.

requirements.title: Battery fire risk assessment
requirements.text_ieee830: Applicant shall assess lithium battery fire risk from UAS battery or fuel leakage on ground. Assessment shall quantify: battery cell capacity (Wh), thermal runaway risk probability, potential fire-spread radius given fuel quantity on aircraft. Applicant shall require certification that batteries meet IEC 62619 (lithium safety standard) and shall document emergency fire-suppression procedures (type of extinguisher, crew training on use). Applicant shall confirm crash-landing zones (planned emergency-landing locations) are clear of vegetation/structures where fire could spread uncontrolled.

requirements.title: Lessons-learned workshop
requirements.text_ieee830: Upon trial completion, applicant shall convene Lessons-Learned Workshop within 30 days, including CAA, applicant, crew, and independent technical reviewer. Workshop shall review: (1) all incidents/anomalies logged during trial, (2) unexpected behaviors observed (emergent swarm behaviors, crew workload surprises, equipment failures), (3) deviations from ConOps, (4) regulatory gate performance (were gates meaningful? Did they catch issues?). Workshop shall produce written lessons-learned report (minimum 10 pages) documenting findings, root causes, and recommendations for future BVLOS swarm trials.

requirements.title: Evidence preservation and archival
requirements.text_ieee830: All recorded evidence (telemetry, video, audio, autonomy logs per REQ012) shall be preserved in digital archive: (1) format standardization (CSV for telemetry, MP4 for video, WAV for audio, JSON for decision logs), (2) metadata documentation (flight date, aircraft ID, crew identifiers, trial phase), (3) integrity verification (SHA256 hash of all files, stored separately), (4) retention period: minimum 2 years or until any litigation/investigation closes, whichever is longer. Archive shall be transferred to CAA Safety Investigation department for long-term storage and future reference.

requirements.title: Technical debrief and forensic validation
requirements.text_ieee830: Independent technical reviewer (not involved in trial) shall conduct forensic analysis of recorded evidence to validate: (1) swarm behavior remained within predicted bounds per architecture specification (REQ008), (2) human control was maintained per meaningful-control criteria (REQ010), (3) no safety-critical near-miss scenarios were missed (events detected and logged that could have escalated to accidents). Reviewer shall issue forensic-analysis report documenting findings and confidence level (high/medium/low) in each validation.

requirements.title: Authorization close-out notice
requirements.text_ieee830: CAA Programme Manager shall issue formal Authorization Close-Out Notice specifying: (1) trial authorization is no longer valid and aircraft shall cease operations in trial configuration, (2) evidence has been received and preserved in CAA archive, (3) if applicable, recommendations for future BVLOS swarm trials (e.g., 'swarm de-confliction algorithm validated; acceptable for expanded swarm size in future trials', or 'emergent-behavior deadlock observed; future swarm designs must include deadlock-detection mechanism'). Close-out notice shall be signed by Programme Manager and filed in permanent trial record.

requirements.title: Case cycle time target
requirements.text_ieee830: Workbench shall enforce case-cycle-time target 60 days from submission to authorization decision. Timeline: (1) days 1-5: applicant submission and Quick Gate (FR-003); (2) days 6-15: Thorough Gate (FR-004) and case opening; (3) days 16-40: SORA, safety-case, autonomy reviews (parallel); (4) days 41-50: apportionment negotiation and panel consensus (REQ015-016); (5) days 51-60: authorization approval and public notification (REQ017). If case requires RFC (return for clarification), clock resets upon resubmission. Programme Manager shall monitor case cycle time and escalate any cases exceeding 75 days (25% buffer) to CAA management.

requirements.title: Workbench availability SLA
requirements.text_ieee830: Workbench service shall maintain 99.5% availability (uptime target) during CAA business hours (0900-1700 UK time, Monday-Friday). Availability is measured across all functional components (intake, case management, review interface, decision logging). Planned maintenance windows shall be announced 2 weeks in advance and shall not exceed 4 hours per quarter. Unplanned outages exceeding 2 hours shall be reported to CAA Director within 24 hours with root-cause analysis.

requirements.title: Evidence retrieval SLA
requirements.text_ieee830: Archive service shall support evidence retrieval with SLAs: (1) metadata query (find flight record by date/aircraft/location) returns results within 1 second, (2) evidence retrieval (download telemetry/video/logs) completes within 10 seconds for files <100 GB, (3) forensic analysis access (support post-incident investigation with full evidence access) within 2 hours of request. Compliance with SLAs shall be measured monthly and reported to CAA Director.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA case officer I want to receive a complete Operational Authorisation pack so that I can open a valid case.
ambiguities_detected: vague qualifier 'complete' - lacks acceptance criteria (DeFOSPAM Dorothy-8)
passive voice 'receive' - unclear if applicant submits or system pulls
undefined outcome - what happens when pack is incomplete?
undefined reference 'valid case' - validity criteria not specified
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The Workbench shall, upon applicant submission, execute the Artefact Detector component to validate the Operational Authorisation Pack against the mandatory-artefact list for the classified operation type. The Pack shall be considered complete when all 8 mandatory artefacts (ConOps, SORA, Safety Case, Training Plan, Personnel List, Environmental Assessment, Insurance, Ground Survey) are present, validly formatted (PDF/XLSX/DOCX), and exceed minimum page thresholds: ConOps >= 5 pages, SORA >= 3 pages, Safety Case >= 10 pages. An incomplete pack shall cause the case to enter the return-for-clarification state with explicit specification of missing artefacts. A complete pack shall cause the case to enter the open-case state and advance to SORA Assessment phase.
rewrites.notes: REQ001 conflates two uses of 'complete': (1) application intake completeness (artefact presence) vs (2) evidentiary completeness (REQ012 context). These must be separated. The rewrite operationalizes PackCompleteness per Vibe Scale. Acceptance criteria derived from Vibe meter METER-001 (Quick Gate) and METER-002 (Thorough Gate).

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA SORA assessor I want swarm-aware SORA evidence so that I can set SAIL and OSO requirements defensibly.
ambiguities_detected: undefined term 'swarm-aware' (DeFOSPAM Dorothy-1, confidence 10/10)
vague qualifier 'defensibly' - no criteria for defense (DeFOSPAM Dorothy-3)
undefined 'SORA evidence' - scope not bounded
implicit outcome - what triggers passage vs. return-for-clarification?
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The SORA Assessor shall, when evaluating a BVLOS swarm trial, demand swarm-aware SORA evidence that explicitly accounts for: (1) collective behavior arising from inter-vehicle coordination; (2) coordination dependencies between aircraft; (3) emergent risks (separation loss, deadlock, single-point-of-failure cascades) not present in single-aircraft SORA. Evidence shall be defensible through reference to JARUS SORA v2.5 methodology, documented hazard analysis (FTA or STAMP), and verification against swarm-specific scenarios. Upon evaluation, the assessor shall issue SAIL (Specific Assurance and Integrity Level) and OSO (Operational Safety Objectives) targets that explicitly reference swarm coordination constraints (e.g., 'Separation maintained during coordinated evasion maneuver shall be >= 50 meters horizontally and 20 meters vertically').
rewrites.notes: DeFOSPAM finding: 'swarm-aware' is the 1st critical undefined term. Chris's language file defines swarm-aware as 'methodology or evidence that explicitly accounts for collective behavior.' The rewrite operationalizes this by enumerating the 3 required evidence components. 'Defensibly' is tied to JARUS methodology and documented analysis to make it verifiable.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA safety-case reviewer I want a clear ConOps and ODD so that I can judge nominal and worst-credible behaviour.
ambiguities_detected: vague qualifier 'clear' - no clarity criteria
undefined term 'worst-credible behaviour' (DeFOSPAM Dorothy-5) - credibility boundary undefined
undefined judgment criteria - what makes behavior acceptable or unacceptable?
passive voice 'judge' - no decision outcomes specified
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The Safety Case Reviewer shall receive a Concept of Operations (ConOps) document containing: (1) swarm composition and roles; (2) operational phases with crew decision points; (3) failure modes and contingency responses; (4) nominal behavior thresholds (e.g., 'normal flight plan deviation < 2 degrees, descent rate < 100 ft/min'). The Reviewer shall also receive the Operational Design Domain (ODD) specifying operating conditions (wind speed <= 15 knots, temperature -5°C to +30°C, GPS accuracy better than 5 meters, daylight hours only). Upon review, the Reviewer shall validate that all worst-credible behavior scenarios are addressed by documented crew procedures or containment systems. Worst-credible behavior is defined as the most severe single failure or environmental excursion reasonably foreseeable (e.g., loss of GPS signal, C2 link loss, geofence breach) that the system and crew must survive. The Reviewer shall issue accept, return-for-clarification, or refuse-with-reasons decision.
rewrites.notes: DeFOSPAM finding: 'worst-credible behaviour' lacks a credibility boundary. The rewrite defines it as single-failure assumption (not common-mode or software defects). Operationalizes 'clear' by enumerating required sections per Vibe meter METER-002.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA airspace regulator I want a TDA and consultation package so that I can segregate the trial from other users.
ambiguities_detected: undefined reference 'TDA' - not expanded on first use
vague reference 'consultation package' - contents not specified
passive voice 'segregate' - unclear what mechanism enforces segregation
undefined outcome - decision criteria for approval not stated
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The Applicant shall submit a Temporary Danger Area (TDA) request to the Airspace Regulator specifying: (1) 3D airspace volume (latitude, longitude, altitude bands, time window); (2) NOTAM text (formatted per ICAO Annex 15) for publication to civilian traffic; (3) coordination evidence showing consultation with ATSU (Air Traffic Service Unit), airspace users (e.g., commercial routes), and affected landowners. The Airspace Regulator shall review the TDA and consultation evidence to confirm: (A) TDA boundaries do not overlap with published airways, restricted airspace, or active training routes; (B) ATSU has confirmed capacity to issue NOTAM and monitor boundary compliance; (C) duration and re-entry procedures are documented. Upon approval, the Regulator shall authorize TDA publication. Upon rejection, the Regulator shall specify which elements (3D volume, NOTAM text, or coordination evidence) require revision.
rewrites.notes: REQ004 uses passive voice to hide actors. The rewrite specifies: Applicant submits, Regulator reviews, ATSU enforces. 'Consultation package' is operationalized as documented evidence of consultation with 3 stakeholder groups.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA airspace regulator I want automatic containment so that a fly-away cannot become uncontrolled BVLOS.
ambiguities_detected: undefined term 'automatic containment' (DeFOSPAM Dorothy-4, 5 subcriteria undefined)
undefined reference 'fly-away' - scope not bounded
undefined outcome 'uncontrolled' - what state triggers containment activation?
missing safety logic - geofence breach action not specified (CRITICAL SAFETY)
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The UAS shall be equipped with automatic containment systems that prevent egress beyond the geofence (defined 3D boundary: latitude ±tolerance, longitude ±tolerance, altitude floor/ceiling) independent of C2 link state or onboard autonomy. Containment shall combine: (1) geofence monitor (runs onboard, polling GNSS at 10 Hz minimum); (2) primary containment action (priority: Return-to-Launch if GPS available; fallback: climb to safe altitude and hold). Upon geofence breach detection (aircraft position exceeds boundary by > 10 meters for > 2 seconds), the system shall: (A) trigger containment action within 500 milliseconds; (B) log breach event (timestamp, coordinates, breach direction); (C) notify crew via alert (audio + visual) within 1 second. Containment design shall be validated through software testing (hitl simulation, boundary stress testing) and approved by CAA autonomy reviewer before trial authorization. Failure of containment shall result in mandatory trial suspension and investigation.
rewrites.notes: DeFOSPAM finding: 'Containment' lacks design and verification criteria. Chris's definition includes 'systems combining mechanical, software, and procedural measures.' The rewrite specifies: (1) boundary tolerance (10 m), (2) response time (500 ms), (3) verification approach (software testing + CAA review). This closes the critical safety gap identified in Observation-1.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA spectrum specialist I want authorised monitored C2 and inter-vehicle links so that link loss is survivable and non-interfering.
ambiguities_detected: vague phrase 'link loss is survivable' (DeFOSPAM Flo-1, time-to-loss undefined)
undefined term 'non-interfering' - interference criteria not specified
undefined outcome 'survivable' - what actions constitute survival?
passive voice 'monitored' - who monitors and what triggers alert?
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The Applicant shall obtain spectrum authorization from the Spectrum Regulator for: (1) C2 (Command and Control) link frequency allocation and power limits; (2) inter-vehicle link frequency allocation (for swarm aircraft-to-aircraft coordination). Authorization shall require evidence that link design is: (A) non-interfering: power spectral density meets ITU-R recommendations and is validated through EMC testing; (B) monitored: real-time link quality telemetry (RSSI, BER) is transmitted to ground crew and logged. Upon C2 link loss (signal quality drops below minimum threshold for > 1 second), the aircraft shall execute failover: (1) if GPS available, maintain altitude and execute Return-to-Launch trajectory; (2) if GPS unavailable, climb to safe altitude (500 feet AGL minimum) and hold. Swarm coordination during C2 loss shall rely on inter-vehicle links and pre-programmed contingency responses. Survival time (duration crew can maintain safe state without ground crew C2) shall be demonstrated through simulation and flight test, with documented acceptance criteria (minimum 5 minutes for BVLOS swarm).
rewrites.notes: DeFOSPAM finding: 'Link loss survivable' is ambiguous on time and action. Chris defines it as 'UAS and swarm have procedures and design such that loss of C2 or inter-vehicle comms can be managed without loss of separation or collision.' The rewrite specifies: (1) time threshold (1 second), (2) failover actions (RTL or hold), (3) survival time acceptance (minimum 5 minutes). This closes DeFOSPAM Observation-2 on undefined outcome.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA DAA assessor I want DO-365 / F3442-aligned evidence for each aircraft and coordinated swarm de-confliction so that cooperative and non-cooperative encounters are resolved predictably.
ambiguities_detected: undefined term 'resolved predictably' (DeFOSPAM Paul-1, mechanism unspecified)
vague reference 'de-confliction' - algorithm and coordination method undefined
implicit outcome - what triggers failure vs. acceptable resolution?
undefined scope - 'cooperative' vs 'non-cooperative' encounter definitions needed
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The Applicant shall provide DO-365 (Minimum Operational Performance Standards for Airborne Detect-and-Avoid Systems) and F3442-2 (Standard Practice for Unmanned Aircraft Systems Traffic Management at Low Altitudes) evidence demonstrating: (1) each aircraft has onboard DAA sensor (radar or electro-optical) with minimum detection range of 500 meters, update rate 1 Hz; (2) swarm coordination uses a defined de-confliction algorithm (specify: centralized ground-based, distributed peer voting, or hierarchical lead-decides) with documented decision logic. For cooperative encounters (intruder transmits ADS-B or FLARM signals), the algorithm shall compute relative bearing and range, issue recommended maneuvers (climb, descend, left turn, right turn) via inter-vehicle links, and enforce agreed maneuver. For non-cooperative encounters (intruder does not transmit), the algorithm shall issue crew-advisory maneuver (preferred direction and magnitude) and record decision rationale in logs. Encounter resolution is predictable when: (A) the same encounter geometry always produces the same response; (B) response is deterministic (not probabilistic); (C) all swarm aircraft follow the preferred maneuver within 2 seconds. Failure (collision, loss of separation < 100 meters horizontally) shall trigger incident investigation and trial suspension.
rewrites.notes: DeFOSPAM finding: 'Resolved predictably' is goal without mechanism. Chris defines it as 'Conflicts with other aircraft are resolved deterministically.' The rewrite specifies: (1) algorithm type (3 options), (2) decision logic (maneuver selection), (3) acceptance criterion (determinism, 2-second execution). This closes DeFOSPAM Observation-3.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA autonomy reviewer I want the decision-making architecture airborne limits and emergent-behaviour evidence so that I can assure what the swarm will do collectively.
ambiguities_detected: undefined reference 'decision-making architecture' - no architecture option specified
undefined term 'emergent-behaviour' - undesired vs. desired undefined
vague qualifier 'assure' - assurance criteria not stated
undefined outcome - what triggers accept vs. return-for-clarification?
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The Applicant shall document the swarm decision-making architecture, specifying which of three design patterns applies: (1) Centralized (all decisions made by ground crew or ground-based autonomy agent); (2) Distributed (peer voting: each aircraft shares state, majority vote selects action); (3) Hierarchical (lead aircraft decides, followers execute). The documentation shall specify: (A) decision authority and override rules; (B) communication topology (mesh, star, line); (C) failure modes (leader loss, communication loss, Byzantine aircraft). The Applicant shall provide emergent-behavior evidence through: (1) formal verification (model checking or theorem proving) proving absence of undesired emergent behaviors (separation loss, deadlock, oscillation); (2) Monte Carlo simulation (minimum 1,000 trials per scenario) demonstrating behavior variance across environmental noise and sensor uncertainty; (3) HITL testing of swarm behaviors (formation flight, coordinated evasion, loss-of-leader recovery). For each scenario, the applicant shall bound behavior variance (e.g., 'separation loss occurs in < 0.1% of trials'). Upon review, the Autonomy Reviewer shall issue accept (architecture and emergent behavior acceptably bounded), return-for-clarification (simulation insufficient or gap identified), or refuse-with-reasons (unacceptable risk).
rewrites.notes: DeFOSPAM finding: 'Swarm decision-making architecture' requires assurance but does not specify architecture. Chris defines it as 'Design of how collective decisions are made.' The rewrite operationalizes by: (1) enumerating 3 architecture types, (2) specifying failure-mode analysis, (3) requiring formal + simulation + test evidence. 'Emergent behavior' is operationalized through variance bounding per Chris's definition.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA AI/ML assurance specialist I want an AMLAS / SACE case a frozen-model manifest and explainability evidence so that ML components are assurable and forensically explainable.
ambiguities_detected: undefined term 'forensically explainable' (DeFOSPAM Dorothy-6, perfect explainability infeasible)
undefined term 'frozen-model manifest' (DeFOSPAM Dorothy-7, 7 subcriteria undefined)
vague phrase 'explainability evidence' - methods not specified
missing conjunction - 'AMLAS / SACE case a frozen-model manifest' uses incorrect grammar, roles unclear
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The Applicant shall provide three artifacts for ML Assurance: (1) AMLAS Case (Assurance Case for Machine Learning in Autonomous Systems per Assure ML CTC) documenting: safety requirements, training data governance, model architecture, robustness testing, and failure modes; (2) Frozen-Model Manifest specifying for each ML model deployed: version ID, training dataset hash (SHA256), hyperparameters (learning rate, regularization), performance metrics (validation accuracy, F1 score for safety-critical classes), operational constraints (input domain, confidence thresholds), and explicit confirmation that model weights and architecture shall remain immutable throughout trial duration; (3) Explainability Evidence demonstrating post-hoc explainability through: feature importance analysis (SHAP, LIME), decision-tree approximations of learned decision boundaries, or mechanistic interpretability (for small models). Forensic explainability is not perfect reconstruction (deemed infeasible for deep learning) but rather: sufficient to identify which input features contributed to a decision, sufficient to identify if model was operating within training distribution, and sufficient to trace decision to model version per Frozen-Model Manifest. Upon review, the ML Assurance Specialist shall verify AMLAS case completeness, confirm Frozen-Model Manifest immutability via code review, and validate explainability methods against discovery standards.
glossary_terms_used: AMLAS (Assurance Case for Machine Learning in Autonomous Systems)
rewrites.notes: DeFOSPAM finding: 'Forensically explainable' is undefined and unrealistic (deep learning post-hoc explainability is not perfect). Chris defines it as 'reconstructable through model weights, training data, decision logic.' The rewrite operationalizes by: (1) specifying 3 techniques (SHAP, LIME, mechanistic), (2) defining forensic explainability as sufficient for investigation, not perfect reconstruction, (3) tying explainability to Frozen-Model Manifest. 'Frozen-model manifest' is now operationalized with 7 required fields per Dorothy-7.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA human factors inspector I want workload duty and take-over evidence so that meaningful human control is demonstrated throughout.
ambiguities_detected: undefined term 'meaningful human control' (DeFOSPAM Dorothy-2, no measurable criteria)
undefined terms 'workload duty' and 'take-over' - thresholds not specified
vague qualifier 'demonstrated' - what constitutes evidence?
temporal ambiguity 'throughout' - frequency of assessment undefined
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The Applicant shall demonstrate meaningful human control through: (1) continuous crew situational awareness (crew maintains awareness of swarm state: position, airspeed, altitude, link status, autonomous decisions) via frequent updates (minimum 1 per second for each aircraft); (2) retained authority (crew retains ability to override autonomous decisions and issue manual commands at all times, with override latency < 2 seconds); (3) intervention capability (crew can perceive off-nominal events and intervene before safety-critical outcomes with time window >= 5 seconds for evasion maneuvers, >= 10 seconds for return-to-launch). Workload evidence shall quantify crew demands (number of concurrent decisions, decision frequency, time budget available) per flight phase and scenario. Crew workload shall not exceed 75% of maximum cognitive capacity during nominal phases, 85% during contingency. Takeover evidence shall demonstrate: (A) time-to-takeover (crew can resume full manual control within X seconds of decision, where X is defined per flight phase); (B) takeover training (crew has conducted minimum N practice takeovers in HITL simulator); (C) takeover authority (crew has full 6-degree-of-freedom control of swarm upon takeover). These measures shall be verified through: pre-flight workload assessment (NASA-TLX or similar), simulator testing, and flight test with independent observer documenting crew actions and response times.
rewrites.notes: DeFOSPAM finding: 'Meaningful human control' lacks measurable criteria. Chris defines it as 'Active, informed crew awareness and retained authority to perceive system state, understand autonomous actions, and intervene before safety-critical decisions.' The rewrite operationalizes through: (1) frequency of updates (1 Hz), (2) override latency (< 2 s), (3) intervention time window (>= 5-10 s), (4) workload thresholds (75-85%), (5) measurement methods (NASA-TLX, simulator, observer). This closes DeFOSPAM Dorothy-2.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA flight-ops inspector I want independent termination a contingency matrix and ATSU coordination so that the trial can be made safe in any off-nominal state.
ambiguities_detected: undefined term 'independent termination' (DeFOSPAM, mechanism/time-to-effect undefined)
undefined term 'contingency matrix' - format and coverage undefined
grammatical error in 'want independent termination a contingency matrix' - missing conjunction
undefined phrase 'made safe' - safety outcome criteria not specified
missing outcome - post-termination actions undefined
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The Applicant shall implement Independent Flight Termination (IFT) system capable of forcing immediate cessation of flight independent of C2 link, inter-vehicle links, or onboard autonomy. The IFT shall: (1) trigger via dedicated ground-based RF kill-switch (crew-operated) or onboard geofence breach (automatic); (2) effect termination within 500 milliseconds via engine cutoff or parachute deployment; (3) operate when all other links are lost (fail-safe design); (4) be verified through ground testing and flight test with multiple failure-mode scenarios. Upon IFT activation, crew shall: (A) immediately alert ATSU and other airspace users via radio; (B) preserve all telemetry and decision logs (5 seconds pre-termination, during, and 30 seconds post); (C) recover or locate all aircraft and evidence; (D) initiate incident reporting if required. The Applicant shall provide a Contingency Matrix (table format) mapping off-nominal states to response procedures. Off-nominal states include: GPS loss, C2 link loss, geofence breach, airspace intrusion, crew incapacitation, loss of separation, low battery. For each state, the matrix shall specify: autonomous action (Return-to-Launch, climb and hold, autonomous evasion), crew procedure (override authority, timing, fallback), and termination trigger (criteria for initiating IFT). ATSU coordination evidence shall document: pre-trial briefing (ATSU understands trial operations and contingency procedures), real-time monitoring (ATSU monitors TDA and notifies crew of airspace conflicts), and emergency procedure (ATSU can declare TDA closed and issue immediate landing directive if safety threshold exceeded).
rewrites.notes: DeFOSPAM finding: 'Independent termination' undefined on mechanism and time-to-effect. 'Contingency matrix' undefined on format. The rewrite specifies: (1) IFT mechanism (RF kill-switch + onboard trigger), (2) response time (500 ms), (3) matrix format (table with 3 columns), (4) off-nominal state enumeration (7 examples), (5) ATSU coordination procedures. This closes DeFOSPAM Observations 4-5.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA flight safety investigator I want complete recorded evidence accepted MOR obligations and a DPIA so that any incident is investigable and privacy is protected.
ambiguities_detected: ambiguous use of 'complete' (DeFOSPAM Dorothy-8 - different meaning than REQ001)
undefined term 'complete recorded evidence' - scope and sampling undefined
grammatical error - 'want complete recorded evidence accepted MOR obligations' (missing verb/conjunction)
undefined outcome - MOR trigger criteria and timing undefined
undefined reference 'DPIA' - scope of personal data undefined
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The Applicant shall implement telemetry and logging systems to record comprehensive evidence throughout trial. Evidence shall include: (1) aircraft state (position, velocity, altitude, attitude) at 10 Hz; (2) autonomy decisions (command issued, decision rationale) at 1 Hz; (3) C2 and inter-vehicle link quality (RSSI, BER) at 1 Hz; (4) crew actions (control inputs, alerts acknowledged, overrides) logged with timestamp; (5) video (forward-looking camera, downlink video) at minimum 30 fps; (6) audio (crew intercom, radio transmissions) sampled at 16 kHz. All evidence shall be time-synchronized (GPS time reference), stored in tamper-evident containers (cryptographic hash stored separately), and retained for minimum 2 years or until incident investigation closes, whichever is longer. Recorded evidence is complete when all 6 categories are present and continuous throughout flight (< 100 ms gaps are acceptable). The Applicant shall fulfill Mandatory Occurrence Report (MOR) obligations by: (1) identifying MOR-reportable events (accidents, serious incidents, occurrences involving injury, structural damage, loss of separation < 100 meters, unintended geofence breach, autonomous decision override); (2) compiling incident narrative and recorded evidence within 72 hours of event; (3) submitting to CAA Safety Investigation department. The Applicant shall conduct a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) per GDPR Article 35 identifying: (A) personal data collected (crew identities, observer photographs, tracked ground personnel); (B) processing purposes (safety investigation, regulatory oversight); (C) retention periods; (D) data subject rights (access, deletion). DPIA shall document privacy controls (footage anonymization, access restrictions, deletion procedures) and obtain CAA Data Protection Officer review.
rewrites.notes: DeFOSPAM finding: 'Complete' in REQ012 is different from REQ001 - here it means evidentiary completeness (all data types present), not artefact completeness. The rewrite separates these by operationalizing 'complete recorded evidence' as 6 specific telemetry categories with defined sampling rates. MOR trigger is operationalized through enumeration of reportable event types per UK aviation regulations.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA authorisation officer I want insurance and a ground survey so that third-party risk is both transferable and factually grounded.
ambiguities_detected: vague qualifier 'factually grounded' (DeFOSPAM) - verification standard undefined
undefined reference 'third-party risk' - risk calculation method undefined
undefined term 'transferable' - what constitutes insurability?
implicit outcome - what insurance coverage minimums required?
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The Applicant shall obtain third-party liability insurance covering bodily injury, property damage, and environmental harm caused by UAS operations, with minimum coverage limits: 1 million GBP per occurrence, 5 million GBP annual aggregate. Insurance policy shall be underwritten by a CAA-approved insurer and shall not exclude BVLOS or swarm operations. Certificate of insurance shall be provided prior to trial start. The Applicant shall commission an independent Ground Environment Survey assessing the trial area within 10 km radius of planned operations. The Survey shall identify and document: (1) ground infrastructure (power lines, communication towers, railways, roads) with GPS coordinates and criticality classification; (2) population density (buildings, schools, hospitals, campgrounds) by district, with estimated occupancy; (3) environmental sensitivity (water sources, protected habitats, cultural heritage) per regulatory register; (4) noise-sensitive receptors (residences, hospitals, schools) identified by occupancy; (5) wind patterns and terrain features affecting aircraft behavior. Survey shall be conducted by licensed surveyor or environmental consultant and signed with certification that findings are factually grounded (based on observed site conditions, not theoretical models). For each identified ground hazard, the applicant shall specify mitigation (reduced altitude, noise baffles, flight path adjustment, observer deployment) or risk acceptance rationale. The Authorisation Officer shall verify that insurance adequately covers identified third-party risks and that ground survey identifies all material hazards in trial corridor.
rewrites.notes: DeFOSPAM finding: 'Factually grounded' is qualitative criterion. Chris defines it as 'Based on verified data and evidence rather than assumptions or theoretical models.' The rewrite operationalizes by requiring licensed surveyor certification and enumeration of 5 survey elements (infrastructure, population, environmental sensitivity, noise receptors, wind patterns). Insurance minimums operationalized through specific GBP amounts (1M per occurrence, 5M aggregate).

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA licensing inspector I want evidenced competency so that the trial is flown by qualified crew.
ambiguities_detected: undefined term 'evidenced competency' - evidence types and standards undefined
vague qualifier 'qualified' - qualifications for BVLOS swarm not specified
undefined reference 'crew' - scope and roles unclear
implicit outcome - what constitutes acceptable evidence?
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The Applicant shall provide documented crew qualifications demonstrating competency across three domains: (1) Airmanship: minimum private pilot license or equivalent, current medical certificate, minimum 500 flight hours (VLOS or BVLOS); (2) BVLOS Swarm Operations: completion of approved training program (minimum 20 hours instruction) covering swarm behavior, coordination failures, contingency procedures, and manual takeover; (3) System-Specific Knowledge: completion of type-specific training for the designated UAS model covering launch/recovery procedures, autonomy modes, C2 link management, and emergency procedures. Evidence of competency shall be documented through: (A) Training Records: signed certificates from approved training organization, dated within 2 years; (B) Examination: scored assessment (minimum 80% pass) covering swarm operations, contingency procedures, and system knowledge; (C) Simulator Practice: minimum 10 practice sessions in HITL simulator, each logged with date, scenario, and instructor evaluation; (D) Flight Experience: logbook entries documenting BVLOS or swarm flights with designated supervising pilot if required. The Licensing Inspector shall review evidence and issue: (1) Permit (all requirements met, crew approved to conduct designated trial); (2) Conditional Permit (requirements substantially met but one area requires remedial training); (3) Deny (material gaps in qualifications). Conditional Permit requires follow-up demonstration before trial commencement.
rewrites.notes: DeFOSPAM finding: 'Evidenced competency' undefined. The rewrite operationalizes competency across 3 domains (Airmanship, BVLOS Swarm, System-Specific) and specifies 4 evidence types (Training Records, Examination 80%, Simulator 10 sessions, Flight Experience). 'Qualified' is now bounded to specific credential types and retention periods.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA/MAA liaison I want a signed apportionment so that regulatory ownership is gap-free.
ambiguities_detected: undefined term 'signed apportionment' - content and format undefined
vague phrase 'gap-free regulatory ownership' - what constitutes gap?
undefined reference 'CAA/MAA liaison' - decision authority unclear
missing outcome - approval/rejection criteria undefined
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: When a trial involves classified MOD (Ministry of Defence) evidence or military applications, both CAA (Civil Aviation Authority) and MAA (Military Aviation Authority) shall co-authorize the trial. The Applicant shall document a Regulatory Apportionment Matrix specifying: (1) which regulatory requirements each authority owns (CAA: airspace segregation, civilian safety, public notification; MAA: security classification, military airspace, sensitive technology export control); (2) decision dependencies (which authority approves first); (3) information barriers (which evidence is classified vs. shareable); (4) escalation procedures (if CAA and MAA requirements conflict). The Apportionment Matrix shall be reviewed and signed by designated representatives of both CAA and MAA, confirming: (A) all regulatory requirements (REQ001-REQ020) have been assigned to at least one authority; (B) no requirement is left unassigned (gap-free coverage); (C) authorities acknowledge their approval responsibilities and authorities to enforce conditions. Regulatory ownership is gap-free when every requirement REQ001-REQ020 is explicitly owned by either CAA or MAA (or jointly), and both authorities have signed. Upon execution, the CAA/MAA liaison shall document approval in the trial file and establish joint oversight mechanisms (e.g., shared evidence portal, joint review gates).
rewrites.notes: REQ015 uses vague language 'signed apportionment' and 'gap-free regulatory ownership.' The rewrite operationalizes by specifying: (1) matrix content (3 columns), (2) approval signature requirement, (3) gap-free definition (every REQ001-020 assigned), (4) joint oversight mechanisms. This closes the ambiguity.

rewrites.original_text: As a CAA programme manager I want phased gates so that evidence builds progressively and is reversible.
ambiguities_detected: undefined reference 'phased gates' - phases and decision criteria undefined
vague qualifier 'reversible' - what triggers reversal?
undefined outcome - progression/reversal decision criteria not specified
implicit assumption - trial structure (3 phases) not formally stated
rewrites.rewrite_ieee830: The trial shall be structured in three reversible phases, each with entry and exit gates controlled by CAA Programme Manager: Phase 1 (VLOS Baseline): single aircraft, visual control, crew within 500 meters. Gate criteria: approval of ConOps, SORA assessment, insurance, crew credentials. Phase 2 (BVLOS Single): transition to BVLOS operations, 2+ aircraft, autonomous waypoint navigation, C2 link reliance. Gate criteria: Phase 1 successful completion (minimum 10 flights, zero incidents), BVLOS-specific equipment validation, C2 link reliability demonstration (99.5% uptime in Phase 1). Phase 3 (BVLOS Swarm): full multi-aircraft coordinated operations, inter-vehicle link coordination, distributed decision-making. Gate criteria: Phase 2 successful completion (minimum 20 flights, zero off-nominal unrecovered events), swarm decision-making validation, emergent-behavior bounded (< 0.1% separation-loss occurrence).
